segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The ledge narrowed between two wind-carved pillars.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She was already there, leaning where the light fell best, as if the mountain had practiced with her beforehand.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Night-silk hair. Violets for eyes, not color so much as focus: not where you were, but where you looked from. Her clothes were court-sin applied to travel: layered, impractical, and somehow no worse for the walk.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She carried almost nothing and yet the air around her moved on a slightly different beat, like a ripple behind the rhythm.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "I've been tracking something hot all morning,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she said, and let her gaze travel very deliberately from the obsidian plate pulsing in Caelin's forearm to his mouth.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik fumbled his striker. tink tink tink.

segments.speaker: elowen
segments.text: "That's a pressure fuse "

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he squeaked, and dove to catch it.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex slid her eyes past Caelin.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Let me guess another follower of the... whatever we're following."

segments.speaker: elowen
segments.text: "Something like that, darling,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: the woman purred.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Don't call me darling,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex said, already bored.

segments.speaker: elowen
segments.text: "Call me Nyx everyone does when they want something,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: the woman said, smiling.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Or Nyxara if you're feeling ceremonious."

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "I'll stick with Nix,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex said.

segments.speaker: elowen
segments.text: "Short for no."

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Mmm. Most men beg to make it yes by the end."

segments.speaker: elowen
segments.text: "Lucky I'm not most men."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana's scowl could have salted meat. Her hand drifted to her hilt by reflex. Elowen passed without pausing, humming the tune she used to keep herself steady. Durgan didn't look at Nyxara at all; he looked past her as if measuring the ledge for exits.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The plate in Caelin's arm lifted a faint silver shimmer magic near magic, attention not threat. No black-violet bloom. Just the world noticing.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik s device clicked twice the particular pattern it made when registering magic it hadn t catalogued before. Warlock, he said quietly, more to himself than anyone. Bound talent. She s drawing from something old. Something that made a bargain before she was born.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "Patrons grow restless when old covenants stir,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nyxara said lightly.

segments.speaker: elowen
segments.text: "Nine arts, nine lights such pretty math. I follow the sums."

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "We're not hiring,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex told her.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "Good,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nyxara said.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "I'm terribly expensive."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik had his striker back in its pouch.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "Do patrons always rehearse entrances, or is that just "

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He stopped himself before saying something he'd regret.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Nyxara,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he corrected briskly.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nyxara's smile didn't change, but the air cooled by a degree.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "Entrances are for the audience."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She nodded at the arch.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "The mountain has excellent taste."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin weighed the two options: a knife at their backs or one where he could see the blade. The scale kept a tightened pulse not warning, not ease.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He thought about Durgan's words from days ago: I can walk with you, or I can follow you. If I follow, you won't know where I am when you sleep.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The logic was the same here.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "Walk with us,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he said, keeping his voice neutral.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "It's a long way to fall alone."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The threat was there, unspoken but clear. Nyxara heard it and her smile widened not offended, amused.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "Safer for everyone,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he added.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Wonderful,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex murmured.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "A complication with cheekbones."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana set the terms, flat and formal.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Distance at night. Truth by day."

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "Daylight forgives many sins,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nyxara said, stepping off the prettiest light to prove she could.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "I accept."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They filed past along the narrow path. Nyxara fell in two places back from Caelin, exactly where her perfume could find him and his eyes could find her. Durgan drifted to the tail without being told.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He turned an empty palm up to the sun for a heartbeat, as if testing heat or habit, then closed it again. His shadow lay tidy at his feet.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin didn't relax. The plate stayed warm. The mountain waited.
